info.title: KISS API Reference
info.description: This reference covers the endpoints most integrations use. For the complete API surface, see the [full API spec](https://app.keepitsimplestorage.com/docs/api).

get.description: Liveness probe. Returns `200` when the API is up.

post.description: Record a lock event (open or close: success, failure, or blocked) after an NFC interaction.
Record an entry-point event (gate or door) after an NFC interaction.

get.description: Fetch a single unit by its KISS `unit_id` (ULID).
Returns every unit in your company with the mapping between your `crm_unit_id` and the KISS `unit_id`. Supports conditional requests via `ETag` / `If-None-Match`.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for deduplication (1–255 chars, 24 h window)

patch.description: Upsert up to 500 units in one idempotent call, matched on your `crm_unit_id`. Unknown IDs create units; known IDs update them. Per-item failures come back in `data.errors` with a `200` response.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for deduplication (1–255 chars, 24 h window)

put.description: Set the unit's single primary user (the owner): marks it occupied, clears any lockout, and optionally creates or updates that user's identity so they can claim the unit in the app. If the unit already has a primary user, this REPLACES them — the prior primary link is overwritten (any guest / secondary accessors are left attached). Adding a guest is a separate flow (coming via Access Grants), not this endpoint.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for deduplication (1–255 chars, 24 h window)

delete.description: End the primary tenancy and reset the unit to vacant (no request body). Clears occupied, the primary-user link, pms_tenant_id, move_in_date, balance_due (to 0), paid_through_date, pms_lockout, pms_auction, pms_unrentable, and pms_status_raw, and detaches secondary accessors. Returns 404 for an unknown unit.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique request identifier for deduplication (1–255 chars, 24 h window)

patch.description: Sparse update of a unit's access facts (overlock, exemption, auction, unrentable, balance). Send only the fields that changed.

occupied.description: Tenancy fields belong to PUT/DELETE /tenancy — reject explicitly

key.description:
Only the device open/close actions a client reports; server-generated
keys are written by their own flows, not forgeable through this endpoint.

zone_id.description:
Accepted for client parity with V1 (the apps may send it); the log is
recorded against the entry point's resolved zone, so it is not yet used.

key.description:
Only the device open/close actions a client reports. Server-generated
keys (registration, bundle, access-share) are written by their own
flows and must not be forgeable through this endpoint.

StorePaymentRequest.description:
Validates a V2 pay request. `type` selects which charges are settled (the
write-side mirror of the 342 invoice-read `filter[type]`); `period` applies
only to advance-pay. Card fields mirror V1 exactly — the PMS is the processor.
StorePaymentRequest.title: StorePaymentRequest

email.description:
password grant (manager). No exists: rule — the controller returns a
generic invalid-credentials response so the endpoint can't be used to
enumerate which emails exist.

errors.description: A detailed description of each field that failed validation.
